evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Client-side posthog-js init, useFeatureFlagEnabled, PostHogProvider, and posthog-python Posthog().identify/capture are all internally consistent and match known PostHog API patterns. However, server-side posthog_client.evaluate_flags(...).is_enabled(...) is non-standard (actual API is get_feature_flag / feature_enabled), and sync_mode kwarg is suspicious. Token names use _PROJECT_TOKEN whereas PostHog conventionally calls it project API key, minor.
evidence.executability: Code blocks parse. Backend main.py uses datetime.now() without importing datetime — runtime NameError. Optional flag endpoint uses likely-wrong method names. main.jsx has 'defaults: 2026-01-30' which is unusual but not fatal. No sandbox-infeasible patterns.
evidence.completeness: All six pieces present: install commands, env templates for both sides, backend code, frontend code, test curl/dev commands, key acquisition steps. Env var names consistent between listed and referenced. Minor duplication in Common Pitfalls.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Multiple official posthog.com/docs URLs anchored inline near relevant code (e.g., [1][3] near init, [12] near python). No fabricated domains.

rubric_notes: docs_unavailable=true; applied long-tail handling. Internally consistent with version claim (posthog==7.16.1). Backend signup handler missing datetime import is the main executability blocker.
